emails.subject: MG : Russian Prepayment Exposure
emails.body: Interesting information floating up from some detailed review work by Tim says a lot about the way MG do business I think. RE NY we should ask the same questions what prepayments exist Philip Bacon seemed uncomfortable with the need for a DASH for each prepayment on a contract add to this the absence of credit people in NY and we may have some issues although Marcelo and others stressed the fact that Concentrates appeared to move no product without a letter of credit, which was then often discounted to realise cash!! Shona we should also discuss the legal contract issue another comment yesterday from Philip was that each of his concentrates contracts were 'unique' whilst I can accept that each may be tailored to customer preferences they still need to be CACSed we should look at the enforceability/legal risk here. Whilst information prepayment risk for Metals should come from London accessing any relevent inofrmation from NY may be helpful Will call later ---------------------- Forwarded by Mike Jordan/LON/ECT on 27/07/2000 09:33 Tim Poullain-Patterson 26/07/2000 21:53
